title: AIDRA — Constraint Profiles
description: Performance comparison under space-grade hardware constraint profiles.

list.name: model
list.label: Model
list.description: Hardcoded list (custom) — public dashboards do not run query-type template vars.

list.name: image_id
list.label: Image ID
list.description: Hardcoded list (custom) — public dashboards do not run query-type template vars.

options.content:
HOW TO READ THIS DASHBOARD
The same Sentinel-1 scene processed by the same model under five simulated hardware budgets (ground → sat-extreme). Pick a Model and Image ID above. Each panel includes a tooltip explaining what the numbers actually mean — including where the simulation is hard (cgroup-style) and where it is soft (duty cycling). The Pass/Fail table at the bottom is the canonical evidence row for each profile.

panels.title: Inference latency per profile
panels.description: Wall-clock inference time over the same Sentinel-1 scene under each hardware profile. ground / sat-high / sat-mid stay close because the inference loop is single-threaded and cannot benefit from extra cores. sat-low (target 0.5) and sat-extreme (target 0.25) take ~2x and ~4x ground respectively, driven by the soft duty-cycle CPUThrottle.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    constraint_profile AS profile,
    ROUND((AVG(inference_ms) / 60000.0)::numeric, 2) AS "avg_latency_min"
FROM execution_log
WHERE (LEFT('$image_id', 1) = '$' OR image_id = '$image_id')
    AND (LEFT('$model', 1) = '$' OR model_name = '$model')
    AND status = 'success'
    AND (notes IS NULL OR notes NOT LIKE '%methodology:pre-321de6b%')
GROUP BY constraint_profile
ORDER BY CASE constraint_profile
    WHEN 'ground' THEN 1
    WHEN 'sat-high' THEN 2
    WHEN 'sat-mid' THEN 3
    WHEN 'sat-low' THEN 4
    WHEN 'sat-extreme' THEN 5
END

panels.title: Peak resident memory per profile
panels.description: Peak RSS observed during inference. NOT a hard limit: the previous setrlimit-based enforcement killed the host process when PyTorch + CUDA shared libraries mapped beyond the budget, so memory is now measured and budget breaches are recorded in execution_log.notes (see the Pass/Fail panel below). Counter-intuitive ordering (sat-low lower than sat-mid; sat-extreme highest) is real: the CPUThrottle pauses let GC run on the throttled profiles, while sat-extreme accumulates buffers during long sleeps.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    constraint_profile AS profile,
    ROUND(AVG(peak_ram_mb)::numeric, 1) AS avg_ram_mb
FROM execution_log
WHERE (LEFT('$image_id', 1) = '$' OR image_id = '$image_id')
    AND (LEFT('$model', 1) = '$' OR model_name = '$model')
    AND status = 'success'
    AND (notes IS NULL OR notes NOT LIKE '%methodology:pre-321de6b%')
GROUP BY constraint_profile
ORDER BY CASE constraint_profile
    WHEN 'ground' THEN 1
    WHEN 'sat-high' THEN 2
    WHEN 'sat-mid' THEN 3
    WHEN 'sat-low' THEN 4
    WHEN 'sat-extreme' THEN 5
END

panels.title: Detections per profile (output stability check)
panels.description: Number of vessel detections per constraint profile. FP32 deterministic runs should match across profiles; dynamic INT8 can diverge by runtime/profile (R12), so this panel exposes output drift instead of hiding it. Any FP32 divergence is a fail.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    constraint_profile AS profile,
    ROUND(AVG(num_detections)::numeric, 1) AS avg_detections
FROM execution_log
WHERE (LEFT('$image_id', 1) = '$' OR image_id = '$image_id')
    AND (LEFT('$model', 1) = '$' OR model_name = '$model')
    AND status = 'success'
    AND (notes IS NULL OR notes NOT LIKE '%methodology:pre-321de6b%')
GROUP BY constraint_profile
ORDER BY CASE constraint_profile
    WHEN 'ground' THEN 1
    WHEN 'sat-high' THEN 2
    WHEN 'sat-mid' THEN 3
    WHEN 'sat-low' THEN 4
    WHEN 'sat-extreme' THEN 5
END

panels.title: Average CPU usage per profile
panels.description: Sum of CPU time across all process threads, expressed as a percentage of one core. ground / sat-high / sat-mid all show ~371% because the OpenMP worker threads PyTorch creates inherit affinity at thread-creation time — applying sched_setaffinity later does NOT migrate them to fewer cores. Only the soft CPUThrottle reduces effective utilisation, which is why sat-low (target 0.5) drops to ~186% and sat-extreme (target 0.25) to ~166%. Hard sub-core enforcement would require cgroup v2 delegation or a Docker side-car.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    constraint_profile AS profile,
    ROUND(AVG(cpu_usage_pct)::numeric, 1) AS avg_cpu_pct
FROM execution_log
WHERE (LEFT('$image_id', 1) = '$' OR image_id = '$image_id')
    AND (LEFT('$model', 1) = '$' OR model_name = '$model')
    AND status = 'success'
    AND (notes IS NULL OR notes NOT LIKE '%methodology:pre-321de6b%')
GROUP BY constraint_profile
ORDER BY CASE constraint_profile
    WHEN 'ground' THEN 1
    WHEN 'sat-high' THEN 2
    WHEN 'sat-mid' THEN 3
    WHEN 'sat-low' THEN 4
    WHEN 'sat-extreme' THEN 5
END

panels.title: Profile Budget Verdict
panels.description: Strict evidence row per profile: observed RAM/CPU is compared to the declared hardware budget, not only to whether the run completed.

targets.rawSql:
WITH budgets(profile, cpu_budget_cores, ram_budget_mb) AS (
    VALUES
        ('ground', 4.0, 24576),
        ('sat-high', 2.0, 4096),
        ('sat-mid', 1.0, 2048),
        ('sat-low', 0.5, 1024),
        ('sat-extreme', 0.25, 512)
), runs AS (
    SELECT
        constraint_profile AS profile,
        ROUND((AVG(inference_ms) FILTER (WHERE status = 'success'))::numeric, 1) AS avg_latency_ms,
        ROUND((AVG(peak_ram_mb) FILTER (WHERE status = 'success'))::numeric, 1) AS avg_ram_mb,
        ROUND((AVG(cpu_usage_pct) FILTER (WHERE status = 'success'))::numeric, 1) AS avg_cpu_pct,
        ROUND((AVG(num_detections) FILTER (WHERE status = 'success'))::numeric, 1) AS avg_detections,
        ROUND((AVG(avg_confidence) FILTER (WHERE status = 'success'))::numeric, 3) AS avg_confidence,
        SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS successes,
        SUM(CASE WHEN status != 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failures
    FROM execution_log
    WHERE (LEFT('$image_id', 1) = '$' OR image_id = '$image_id')
        AND (LEFT('$model', 1) = '$' OR model_name = '$model')
        AND (notes IS NULL OR notes NOT LIKE '%methodology:pre-321de6b%')
    GROUP BY constraint_profile
)
SELECT
    b.profile,
    b.ram_budget_mb,
    r.avg_ram_mb,
    ROUND((r.avg_ram_mb / NULLIF(b.ram_budget_mb, 0))::numeric, 2) AS ram_budget_ratio,
    b.cpu_budget_cores,
    ROUND((r.avg_cpu_pct / 100.0)::numeric, 2) AS observed_cores,
    r.avg_latency_ms,
    r.avg_detections,
    r.avg_confidence,
    COALESCE(r.successes, 0) AS successes,
    COALESCE(r.failures, 0) AS failures,
    CASE
        WHEN r.profile IS NULL THEN 'NO DATA'
        WHEN r.failures > 0 THEN 'FAIL_RUN'
        WHEN r.avg_ram_mb > b.ram_budget_mb THEN 'FAIL_RAM'
        WHEN (r.avg_cpu_pct / 100.0) > (b.cpu_budget_cores * 1.10) THEN 'WARN_CPU'
        WHEN r.avg_confidence < 0.5 THEN 'DEGRADED'
        ELSE 'PASS'
    END AS budget_verdict
FROM budgets b
LEFT JOIN runs r ON r.profile = b.profile
ORDER BY CASE b.profile
    WHEN 'ground' THEN 1
    WHEN 'sat-high' THEN 2
    WHEN 'sat-mid' THEN 3
    WHEN 'sat-low' THEN 4
    WHEN 'sat-extreme' THEN 5
END
